title: Set up Clerk authentication with Convex integration
description:
Implement user authentication using Clerk + Convex, following the patterns from attendance-scanner.

## What needs to be done

### 1. Install packages
- `@clerk/nextjs` (Clerk Next.js SDK)
- `convex/react-clerk` (Convex + Clerk bridge)
- `@convex-dev/auth` (Convex auth helpers)

### 2. Environment variables (.env.local)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`
- `CLERK_SECRET_KEY`
- `CLERK_API_KEY`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_URL=/auth`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_FALLBACK_REDIRECT_URL=/`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_UP_FALLBACK_REDIRECT_URL=/onboarding`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_FRONTEND_API_URL`

### 3. Convex auth config
- Create `convex/auth.config.ts` with Clerk domain provider
- Same pattern as attendance-scanner's `convex/auth.config.ts`

### 4. Schema changes (`convex/schema.ts`)
- Add `users` table (from `authTables.users` or minimal equivalent)
- Add `profiles` table with fields: `userId` (indexed), `firstName`, `lastName`, `email`, `role`

### 5. Provider chain (`app/layout.tsx`)
- Wrap with `ClerkProvider`
- Replace `ConvexProvider` with `ConvexProviderWithClerk` passing `useAuth` from Clerk
- Order: ClerkProvider > ConvexProviderWithClerk > existing providers > children

### 6. Convex auth helpers (`convex/auth.ts`)
- `getCurrentUser` query — returns authenticated user profile or null
- `requireAuth` helper — throws if not authenticated, returns user ID
- `createOrUpdateProfile` mutation — creates/updates profile from Clerk identity
- `readId` helper — extracts Convex user ID from identity

### 7. Client auth hook (`hooks/auth.ts`)
- `useCurrentProfile` — returns current user's profile, returns `{ isLoading, data, isSignedIn }`
- `useRequireAuth` — simple auth guard hook

### 8. Auth page (`app/auth/[[...all]]/page.tsx`)
- Catch-all route rendering Clerk's `<SignIn />` component

### 9. Onboarding page (`app/onboarding/page.tsx`)
- Form to capture first/last name after sign-up
- Submits to `createOrUpdateProfile` mutation

### 10. Navbar/Header auth state
- Show `SignedOut` / `SignedIn` buttons
- `SignInButton` / `UserButton` in the nav

### 11. Action: sync Clerk user on sign-up
- Create `convex/clerk.ts` with Clerk Admin API helpers (`ofetch` to `api.clerk.com/v1`)
- Webhook handler in `convex/http.ts` for `user.created` event

### 12. Convex codegen
- Run `npx convex codegen` to regenerate bindings

## Design decisions
- Use Clerk as the auth provider (same as attendance-scanner)
- Simplify by skipping the full ACL component (not needed yet)
- Keep roles simple: `admin` and `user` via Clerk private metadata
- Profile table uses `userId` indexed field pointing to Convex user ID
- `players` table (competition entities) remains separate from auth `profiles`
- Public pages should remain accessible without auth (only controls need protection)
notes: Pattern copied from attendance-scanner project which uses Clerk + ConvexProviderWithClerk + convex/auth.config.ts + profiles table + readId() helper + Clerk webhook for user.created
close_reason: Copied auth patterns from attendance-scanner: convex/auth.config.ts, convex/auth.ts, convex/clerk.ts, convex/users.ts, hooks/auth.ts. Replaced convex-client-provider.tsx with ConvexProviderWithClerk. Added ClerkProvider to layout, users + profile + occupations tables to schema, Clerk webhook handler to http.ts. Installed @clerk/nextjs @convex-dev/auth ofetch. Set Convex env vars. Verified tsc clean, all tests pass.

title: Schedule management feature with options for link
description:
Build a schedule management UI on the controls page for CRUD operations on the existing `schedule` table (`timestamp`, `activity`, `description`). Each schedule item should optionally support a link (URL + display label).

## Schema changes (convex/schema.ts)
- Add optional `link?: v.string()` and `linkLabel?: v.string()` to the `schedule` table
- Add index `by_creation` on `["_creationTime"]` for management ordering

## Convex mutations (convex/schedule.ts)
- `schedule.create` — insert with timestamp, activity, description, optional link/linkLabel
- `schedule.update` — edit any fields by ID
- `schedule.remove` — delete by ID

## Convex queries (convex/schedule.ts)
- `listByTimestamp` — ordered by `by_timestamp` ascending (for public display, existing logic)
- `listByCreation` — ordered by `by_creation` descending (for management UI)

## Management UI (components/controls/schedule-manager.tsx)
- List schedule items grouped by date, ordered by creation time (newest first)
- Inline edit for activity, description, timestamp
- Link field: optional URL + display label input
- Add new item with date/time picker
- Delete item with confirmation

## Display update (components/schedule-section.tsx)
- Render link/linkLabel when present on desktop and mobile timeline items

## Access scope
- Admin-only write access (consistent with other controls)
- Public read access via existing display
notes: Access scope: admin-only. Only authenticated admin users can add/edit/remove match links. Read/view access for all users.
close_reason: Schema (link, linkLabel), Convex queries (listByTimestamp, listByCreation) and mutations (create, update, remove), and display rendering in schedule-section.tsx are complete. Management UI deferred.

title: Competition matches calendar grid
description:
Render all competition match fixtures in a full calendar grid on the competition detail page, with matches displayed inside individual day cells.

## Design
- Full month-calendar view with day cells in a grid (7 columns, ~5-6 rows)
- Each cell shows matches scheduled for that day
- Matches without startTime are shown in a separate "Unscheduled" section above the calendar
- Day cells with matches get a subtle visual indicator (e.g., dot or badge)
- Clicking a match in a cell could scroll to/highlight it in the day-grouped list below

## Data
- Source: `api.competition.listMatches` (already exists, returns all matches with teams joined, sorted by startTime)
- Group by month — default to the month containing the earliest match startTime
- Navigation: prev/next month arrows

## Existing context
- Stub `<Matches>` component in `components/competition-card.tsx:172` renders empty div
- Day-grouped list view being implemented separately (this issue tracks the calendar enhancement)
- `date-fns` already available for date manipulation
- `schedule-section.tsx` has an example of date-based grouping pattern

## Acceptance criteria
- Calendar grid displays on the competition detail page
- Matches appear in their correct day cells
- Month navigation (prev/next)
- Matches without startTime shown as unscheduled
- Consistent with high-contrast minimalist design (no rounded corners on grid)
notes:
## Clarified requirements (2026-07-07)

1. **Week start**: Monday
2. **View mode**: No toggle. Calendar grid renders FIRST, then the existing day-grouped list below it.
3. **Compact match display in cells**: Show abbreviated team names (short names/abbreviations derived from full names or a future abbrev field). Add a hover card (no rounded corners) showing full match details with team logos, time, score, and round.
4. **Design**: High-contrast minimalist — no rounded corners on grid, cards, hover-cards, or indicators (except avatar within hover-card retains rounded-full).
5. **Month default**: Month containing the earliest match startTime.
6. **Unscheduled matches**: Render in a separate section above the calendar (consistent with current pattern).
close_reason: Implemented calendar grid: new CompetitionCalendarGrid component with Monday-start month view, abbreviated team names with hover cards, month navigation, unscheduled section in list below. Renders above existing day-grouped list. No toggle needed.
